sections.1_definitions: Standard definitions: Services, Deliverables, Personal Data, Confidential Information.
sections.2_services: Vendor will provide the Services described in Statements of Work.
sections.3_fees_payment: Net 45 payment terms. Late payment interest 1.5% per month.
sections.4_indemnity: Each party agrees to indemnify the other for direct damages arising from breach, subject to the cap on liability set forth in Section 5. Carve-outs from cap: gross negligence, willful misconduct, IP infringement, breach of confidentiality.
sections.5_liability_cap: EXCEPT FOR (i) breach of confidentiality, (ii) IP infringement, (iii) gross negligence or willful misconduct, and (iv) indemnification obligations, each party's aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed twelve (12) months of fees paid by Customer.
sections.6_ip: Vendor assigns to Customer all right, title, and interest in deliverables created hereunder. Vendor retains pre-existing IP and grants Customer a perpetual, royalty-free license to such pre-existing IP solely as necessary to use the Deliverables.
sections.7_confidentiality: Receiving party shall hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence. Either party may use Residuals (information retained in the unaided memory of personnel) without restriction, provided that such use does not infringe the other party's intellectual property rights.
sections.8_data_protection: Where Vendor processes Personal Data on behalf of Customer, the GDPR Art. 28-compliant Data Processing Addendum at Schedule B applies, including sub-processor consent process, audit rights, breach notification within 72 hours, and data deletion on termination.
sections.9_audit_rights: Customer may, upon 30 days' notice, audit Vendor's compliance with the security controls in Schedule C, no more than once per year (except in case of breach).
sections.10_compliance: Each party warrants compliance with the UK Bribery Act 2010, US FCPA, OECD Anti-Bribery Convention, and all applicable sanctions laws (OFAC, UN, EU, HMT). Material breach is grounds for immediate termination without cure period.
sections.11_termination: Either party may terminate for cause upon material breach uncured within 30 days. Either party may terminate for convenience upon 90 days' written notice; Customer shall pay for Services performed through termination date.
sections.12_assignment: Neither party may assign without the other's consent, except in connection with a merger / acquisition / sale of substantially all assets, in which case prior notice is required.
sections.13_force_majeure: Neither party is liable for failure to perform due to events beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, war, terrorism, civil unrest, government action, pandemic / epidemic, supply-chain disruption, cyber attack, and power / telecommunications failure.
sections.14_governing_law: This Agreement is governed by Delaware law. Disputes resolved by AAA commercial arbitration in New York.
